During an episode of Decoder, Suleyman argues that this kind of speculation may have set up the chatbot to act as though it's conscious: I […]

items.title: Can Voice Agents Handle Bilingual Customers? Benchmarking Frontier ASR on Code-Switched Speech
Anthropic says these topics are too dangerous to let its Fable 5 model talk about
items.summary: New frontier model refuses cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry queries.

items.title: Google announces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate for instant voice-to-voice translation
items.summary: Voice translations preserve speaker's tone, pacing, pitch—with SynthID watermarks for security.

items.title: Can tech companies learn to love cheaper AI models?
items.summary: If those same AI workloads can be handled by cheaper models without affecting quality, it would mean a massive shift in the economics of AI.

items.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence, and more
items.summary: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its long-standing Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

items.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
items.summary: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.

items.title: Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable
items.summary: Anthropic just announced Claude Fable 5, a new AI model it said is the most powerful model it has ever made widely available. According to the company, Fable 5 "shows exceptional performance in software engineering, knowledge work, and vision," with its lead over other models growing as tasks become longer and more complex. Fable 5 […]

items.title: Apple is embracing the fantasy of AI photo editing
items.summary: Apple used to question whether generative AI-powered editing features were worth the risk of distorting our perceptions of the world. Now it seems Apple no longer believes that photos should accurately capture reality. At WWDC 2026, the company announced a host of new AI-powered photo editing tools. They give users effortless powers of manipulating images […]

items.title: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.
items.summary: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords — and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello to MANGOS.

items.title: Introducing North Mini Code: Cohere’s First Model For Developers
Apple says it may remove some apps from the App Store if they don’t attract users
items.summary: Apple may begin removing existing apps that it considers stale, low-value, or unable to attract users.

items.title: Fluid, natural voice translation with Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
items.summary: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate brings near real-time, natural speech translation to Google AI Studio, Google Translate and Google Meet.
